title: The Macro Minute: When will the Fed revise its inflation target higher?

Howdy team 42. It's your skipper here at
Darius Dell to present our macro minute
for Tuesday May 20th 2025. As always,
we'll start with the executive summary
from today's leadoff morning note. If
you would like to review the analysis
supporting these conclusions and to see
if you need to make any changes in your
portfolio to remain on the right side of
market risk, you need to be a client of
42 macro. So today's key macro question
is when will the Fed revise its
inflation target higher? Our answer,
Chinese battery producer cattle's highly
successful capital raise in Hong Kong is
supportive of our view that the US and
China are headed for a somewhat amicable
divorce over the next few years, leaving
behind a multipolar world that may
require a significant reduction in the
US's 26 trillion net international
investment deficit, now an unsettling
twothirds of US GDP and a fifth of
global GDP. In our view, the most
underappreciated upside risk in the
economy and asset markets will be
replacing the current tired line of
orthodox US monetary policy thinking
with fresh ideas. One fresh idea that we
have consistently floated since we
introduced our sticky inflation theme in
January of 2022 is a 100 basis point
positive revision to the Fed's 2%
inflation target. This would allow the
Fed to fill the capital void left behind
by China. Specifically, a 3% inflation
target would accomplish five very
important policy goals for the Trump
administration. Number one, credibility
in the bond market. Number two, a
significant reduction in the policy
rate. Number three, trillions of savings
on net interest. Number four, lower real
interest rates across the curve. And
number five, less crowding out of the US
private sector by Uncle Sam. A Fed that
takes pressure off investors from having
to capitalize the Treasury bond market
frees up trillions in private capital
that can be redeployed into a reflexive
credit cycle for consumers and
businesses left behind by President
Biden's case-shaped paradigm. A a
positive revision to the Fed's
increasingly impractical 2% inflation
target is not an admission of defeat.
Rather, it's an admission of the reality
of populist economics. And the sooner
the Fed accepts reality, the sooner
they'll get on board with supporting the
Trump administration's stated desire for
a golden age for workingclass American
families. Transition to our 42 macro
dashboard. As always, I'll wrap up the
question from our community. This one's
not really a question, but some elements
of the comment that I think are worth
unpacking. So, let me just read it. um
says, "Thank you uh Darius for giving
voice to what frustrates me the most
about how financial and monetary matters
are handled by our elected
representatives. Their inability or will
ignorance to see the forest through the
trees. I hope your message resonates
with the powers to be that be as one of
the many who've been priced out of
liquid asset markets, priced out of
credit markets, and given a limit
playing limited playing field upon which
to grow a business after decades of
negative growth in industrial policy. I
for one am hoping that policy ideals
like yours are given a chance to
succeed. Thank you. Appreciate that. Uh
going back to reading this um says, "I
never wanted to be a tech pro or real
estate tycoon or have anything to do
with financial markets, but these are
the cards I've been dealt. So, I'm
trying to make lemonade out of lemons.
Trying to learn how financial markets
work is much harder than the physical
sciences I learned in university. I've
been only at it for three years and and
and I am extremely grateful for KISS. I
can trust that KISS will continue to
grow my small nest egg while I become
more confident in developing my
investing skills. Thank you, Darius, for
all that you do. Cheers. So, uh, thank
you very much, uh, to the client, um,
for the really kind feedback. Uh, this
is one of the many reasons we built our
completely systematic kiss before
construction process is to, you know,
keep investors invested when it's time
to be invested. Um, and there are times
to not be invested and KISS does a great
job at that as well. Um and um you know
so I'm glad you know and again KISS
keeps you having as an investor retail
investor in particular better outcomes
in your portfolio than you generally
would otherwise experience based on your
level of experience and acumen and
technical knowhow alone. It's investing
is really hard. Maximizing upside
capture bull markets and also minimizing
downside capture in bare markets in a
way that eliminates volatility drag from
your portfolio and allows your portfolio
to compound from higher levels of net
asset value at higher rates for a longer
period of time so that you retire on
time and comfortably is really effing
hard to
do. Everything I just said, rewind
everything I just said for the past 30
seconds. That's a lot of stuff that you
need to do very consistently in order to
retire on time and comfortably. And it
blows my mind that so many people think
that they can do that without, you know,
some some form of assistance. We all
need assistance in life. I don't do my
own teeth. I go to the
dentist. You know, I don't grow my own
corn. I go to the grocery store, right?
You know what I mean? So, we all need to
have a little bit more humility when it
comes to how hard investing is. And
ultimately, I think what KISS does, and
certainly does this for me in my own
portfolio, is it forces us all, all of
us, the thousands of us around the world
that are participating in KISS, it
forces us to have humility. It forces us
to acknowledge that we don't know
everything. It forces us to acknowledge
that we have no pre-ordained right to
knowing the the future is for the
economy and asset markets. And it
ultimately forces that humility
associated with accepting the fact that
the market knows more than us at all
times and just generally allows us to
generally participate in what the
markets are trying to price in uh visav
the um the trend following overlays that
that that that KISS features uh in the
stock gold and bitcoin asset classes.
So, um, super proud of Kiss, super proud
of this client for acknowledging the
value of KS and along their investing
journey. Um, and hopefully we'll
continue to to create really positive
outcomes for them as well. But going
back to the beginning of this, I think
it's really important to kind of um just
quickly unpack, you know, uh you know,
one of the things I feel very compelled
to do uh as someone who grew up on the
bottom 0.1% of the K-shaped US economy,
um as a Christian man, and as someone
who just generally, you know, wants to
see people do do good. As you know,
someone who grew up around people that
weren't doing very good, including the
folks who lived in my
home, God blessed me with a lot of
talent. obviously otherwise I wouldn't
have a business called 42 macro and a
lot of intellect and a lot of
thoughtfulness in order to potentially
at some point help create better policy
outcomes for the people on the better
part of the bottom part of the king and
so uh I'm grateful that we are able to
use our platform to educate the general
investing public but not just the
general investing public we're educating
central bankers we're educating policy
makers we're educating politicians
hopefully educating the media although
they continue to keep their heads buried
at sound like I don't exist outside of
our friends at uh Fox Business. How how
you doing, Charles and Maria.
Um we're going to stay on this mission
for a really long period of time because
I think we are, you know, helping to
change the world uh for better. Uh for
the people that have been left behind,
the people on the bottom part of the K,
the people that grew up, you know,
without, you know, skipping meals and
and and not having lights on like like
we did for for many months many many
months of my childhood. um in and out of
homeless shelters, sleeping in cars. You
know, these are the things that I've
seen with my eyes. And I want to make
sure that America, the folks in America
on the bottom part of K and really the
rest of the world, it's not just an
America thing. Um have an opportunity um
to to to experience a better life for
them and their their families and their
kids. So, um super grateful that uh you
guys don't mind me using my platform to
try to change the world. Um obviously,
we're here. The pime primary goal 42
macro is to help investors retire on
time and comfortably andor to keep our
byside clients uh gainfully employed and
and and earning uh uh exorbitant
bonuses. That's our primary goal. But a
secondary goal and a very important core
secondary goal for me is to educate the
world and and try to use that education,
that thoughtfulness, that wisdom to make
the world a better place. And so um
we're going to keep doing that. If you
guys are supporting of that, uh let us
know. If you don't support that, then we
have the best cancellation policy in the
world. 100% of our clients and canceled
any time without penalty. 100% of them.
You know why? We don't want anyone here
inside of our payw wall that does not
appreciate the immense value that we are
creating in their portfolios and the
immense value that we're creating in
their lives visav helping them learn
these very complicated esoteric you know
high level financial uh and economic
concepts. Uh but also the value that
we're creating in reducing the amount of
stress that they feel uh in their
investing activity. uh and ultimately
giving them time back so they can go
spend more time with their family, more
time doing the Lord's work in their
community and less time, you know,
chugging financial media noise on on
finance Twitter and and listening to a
bunch of finance podcasts um and get
whipped around by a bunch of talking
heads. So, uh hopefully that resonates.
If it doesn't resonate, then who cares?
